title: Dockerfile should only have one ENTRYPOINT and CMD instruction
description: Dockerfile should only have one ENTRYPOINT and CMD instructionAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugThe Dockerfile should contain at most oneENTRYPOINTand oneCMDinstruction, because only the last one will have an effect.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoMultipleENTRYPOINTorCMDinstructions in a file can lead to confusion as we may think they are all applied. This is not the case, as only the last one is applied.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since10.3Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since10.3©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Dockerfile should only have one ENTRYPOINT instruction. Only the last ENTRYPOINT will have an effect.
Dockerfile should only have one CMD instruction. Only the last CMD will have an effect.
